title: Aggressively alive vs Blade-Runner-on-missions, Aug 2024
html:
TL;DR

  • A single-paragraph identity oscillation note. Yahir describes feeling alternately "aggressively alive" and "like the guy from Blade Runner going on missions not exactly knowing why, but still doing them."
  • The seek list within: "Maybe it's a partner, maybe it's great life work, maybe it's letting go of all desire, maybe it's chasing your aspirations so fiercely they have no other option but to manifest."
  • Closing line is the most Yahir-like meta-frame: "The universe is allowing me to live."
  • Written ~2 weeks before he flies out for China — the Blade-Runner-mission state probably tracks to mid-prep limbo.

Key quote

"Sometimes I feel like I am aggressively alive, other times it feels like there is nobody with me and I am just moving thru life."

Why it matters in the graph

  • Cleanest articulation of Yahir's two-state oscillation — feeds directly into the heightened perception trigger (vibrant-colors) and normal neutral to everything concepts. Both states named in one paragraph.
  • The Blade Runner reference is the first explicit cinematic-self-reference in the corpus — pairs with the watchlist taste in 2023 07 15 things to watch (Dark City, Blade-Runner-adjacent sci-fi).
  • "Universe is allowing me to live" is a precursor to the walk with gods eyes line that recurs through 2025–26.

Open questions

  • Which Blade Runner — original Deckard or 2049 K? Tone matters for what "missions you don't know why" means.
  • Were the Aug 2024 doldrums what motivated the rapid China-pivot the next month?
